summary: scripts/migrations/v3_004_normalize_chunk_ids.sql — single-transaction in-place rewrite of every chunk_id reference across nodes / edges / chunk_embeddings / staged_tags / staged_edges, with pre-migration collision audit
description:
## Deliverable

scripts/migrations/v3_004_normalize_chunk_ids.sql — single transaction that updates chunk_id strings in place across every table that references them.

## Tables affected

- nodes — id (PRIMARY KEY) for type='chunk' rows
- edges — source_id, target_id (any chunk-side endpoints, including the 305 [auto] PARALLELS, 90 verified EXPRESSES, 2,067 proposed EXPRESSES, 2,531 BELONGS_TO inferred)
- chunk_embeddings — chunk_id (PRIMARY KEY)
- staged_tags — chunk_id
- staged_edges — source_chunk, target_chunk

## Approach

PRAGMA foreign_keys=OFF inside a transaction (necessary because the FKs from edges/chunk_embeddings/staged_* into nodes(id) are not declared ON UPDATE CASCADE — without disabling, the nodes UPDATE would fail). Apply the per-tradition prefix rewrites in a deterministic order, then PRAGMA foreign_key_check before COMMIT to verify zero orphans.

Per-tradition prefix map (must match scripts/backfill_chunk_ids.py from C1):

    Neoplatonism.        → neoplatonism.
    Egyptian.            → egyptian.
    Taoism.              → taoism.
    Greek Mystery.       → greek_mystery.
    Christian Mysticism. → christian_mysticism.
    Zoroastrianism.      → zoroastrianism.
    Jewish Mysticism.    → jewish_mysticism.
    Buddhism.            → buddhism.
    Mesopotamian.        → mesopotamian.

For each of those 9 prefixes, run:

    UPDATE nodes            SET id           = REPLACE(id,           '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE id           LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';
    UPDATE edges            SET source_id    = REPLACE(source_id,    '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE source_id    LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';
    UPDATE edges            SET target_id    = REPLACE(target_id,    '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE target_id    LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';
    UPDATE chunk_embeddings SET chunk_id     = REPLACE(chunk_id,     '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE chunk_id     LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';
    UPDATE staged_tags      SET chunk_id     = REPLACE(chunk_id,     '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE chunk_id     LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';
    UPDATE staged_edges     SET source_chunk = REPLACE(source_chunk, '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE source_chunk LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';
    UPDATE staged_edges     SET target_chunk = REPLACE(target_chunk, '<DISPLAY>.', '<SNAKE>.') WHERE target_chunk LIKE '<DISPLAY>.%';

## Pre-migration collision audit

Before any UPDATE: SELECT count of would-collide rows for each tradition. Currently:

    Jewish Mysticism: 37 chunks
    jewish_mysticism: 6 chunks       ← if these point at the SAME chunk file, the rewrite produces a PRIMARY KEY conflict on nodes

If any collisions exist, the migration must abort with a SELECT that prints the colliding chunk_ids so the operator can decide (manual reconcile, drop one set, etc.). Audit-only mode: include the colliding-rows SELECT inline above the UPDATEs, behind a comment. Or split into two scripts: an audit .sql and the actual migration .sql.

## Post-migration verification (inside the same transaction, before COMMIT)

    PRAGMA foreign_key_check;  -- must return zero rows
    SELECT 'unmigrated nodes',        COUNT(*) FROM nodes
        WHERE type='chunk' AND id GLOB '*[A-Z]*';  -- catch any remaining capitalization
    SELECT 'unmigrated edges (source)', COUNT(*) FROM edges
        WHERE source_id GLOB '*[A-Z]*' AND source_id NOT LIKE 'concept.%';
    -- ... same for target_id, chunk_embeddings, staged_*
    -- All counts must be zero.

If any check fails, ROLLBACK and report.

## Out of scope

- Changes to schema/corpus-schema.sql (the canonical Postgres schema) — this is a SQLite migration on the operator's working DB; the export artifact will reflect the new IDs naturally on the next export.
- Changes to scripts/export.py — chunk_id is just text passed through; format change is transparent.
